title: Feeling Happy
content: Nila jumped out of bed with a big smile. It was her favourite day. She felt happy and full of energy.
question: How did Nila feel?

title: Feeling Sad
content: Tom's toy broke and would not work anymore. He sat in the corner quietly. A tear rolled down his cheek.
question: Why was Tom sad?

title: Feeling Scared
content: Anya heard a loud noise in the dark. Her heart beat very fast. She hid under her blanket.
question: Why did Anya hide?

title: Feeling Excited
content: Today is the day of the school trip! Ravi woke up early and got dressed fast. He could not stop smiling.
question: Why was Ravi excited?

title: Feeling Proud
content: Maya finished her drawing all by herself. Her teacher put it on the wall. Maya stood tall and felt very proud.
question: Why did Maya feel proud?

title: Feeling Angry
content: Leo wanted the last biscuit but his brother took it. Leo's face turned red. He stamped his foot on the floor.
question: Why was Leo angry?

title: Feeling Calm
content: Priya sat by the window and listened to the rain. She took slow deep breaths. She felt very peaceful and calm.
question: What helped Priya feel calm?

title: Feeling Lonely
content: Zara moved to a new school. She did not know anyone yet. She sat alone and felt lonely.
question: Why did Zara feel lonely?

title: Feeling Brave
content: Sam was scared to climb the big slide. He took a deep breath. He climbed it anyway and felt very brave.
question: What made Sam feel brave?

title: Feeling Surprised
content: Mia walked into a dark room. Then the lights came on. Everyone shouted Surprise! and Mia gasped.
question: How did Mia feel when the lights came on?

title: Helping a Friend
content: Ben saw his friend drop all his books. He helped pick them up. His friend said thank you and smiled.
question: What did Ben do?

title: Sharing is Caring
content: Lily had two sandwiches at lunch. Her friend forgot her food. Lily gave one sandwich to her friend.
question: What did Lily give her friend?

title: A Kind Word
content: Jay was having a bad day. His classmate said: You are doing great! Jay smiled and felt much better.
question: What made Jay feel better?

title: Holding the Door
content: Ella saw an old man walking slowly. She held the door open for him. He nodded and said thank you.
question: What did Ella do?

title: Helping at Home
content: Mum looked very tired after work. Riya set the table for dinner without being asked. Mum gave her a big hug.
question: What did Riya do to help?

title: Cheering a Friend
content: Kai's friend was about to run her first race. Kai cheered loudly from the side. His friend ran her best race ever.
question: What did Kai do for his friend?

title: Saying Sorry
content: Dan bumped into a girl in the hall and her lunch fell. He said sorry right away and helped clean it up.
question: What did Dan do after the bump?

title: Welcoming Someone New
content: A new boy joined the class. No one talked to him. Sana walked over and said hello. They became good friends.
question: What did Sana do?

title: Feeding the Birds
content: Every morning, Dev put seeds on the windowsill. The birds flew down to eat them. Dev loved watching them.
question: What did Dev put on the windowsill?

title: Taking Turns
content: Four friends wanted to play on the swing. They decided to take turns. Each child had a fair turn.
question: How did the friends solve the problem?

title: Morning Routine
content: Every morning, Jin washes his face and brushes his teeth. Then he eats breakfast. He is ready for school by eight o'clock.
question: What does Jin do before breakfast?
options: Washes his face and brushes his teeth
answer: Washes his face and brushes his teeth

title: Packing a Bag
content: Before school, Meera packs her bag. She puts in her books, pencils, and water bottle. Then she checks she has everything.
question: What does Meera put in her bag?

title: Bedtime Routine
content: At night, Chloe puts on her pyjamas and brushes her teeth. She reads one page of her book. Then she turns off the light.
question: What does Chloe do before sleep?
options: Puts on pyjamas and brushes teeth
answer: Puts on pyjamas and brushes teeth

title: Washing Hands
content: After playing outside, Amit always washes his hands. He uses soap and warm water. Then he dries his hands on a towel.
question: When does Amit wash his hands?

title: Choosing Clothes
content: It was cold outside. Priya picked a warm jumper and her thick coat. She put on her gloves too.
question: Why did Priya pick a warm jumper?

title: Eating Lunch
content: At noon, the school bell rang for lunch. Omar took out his lunchbox. He had a sandwich, an apple, and some water.
question: What was in Omar's lunchbox?

title: Tying Shoelaces
content: Nate could not tie his shoes at first. He practiced every day. After one week, he could do it all by himself.
question: How did Nate learn to tie his shoes?

title: Keeping Things Tidy
content: Before bed, Sasha puts her toys in the box. She folds her clothes and puts them on the chair. Her room is always tidy.
question: What does Sasha do with her toys?

title: Crossing the Road
content: Finn always stopped at the kerb. He looked left, then right, then left again. Only when it was safe did he cross the road.
question: What did Finn do at the kerb?
options: Looked left, right, left before crossing
Waited for a car to stop
answer: Looked left, right, left before crossing

title: Going to Sleep
content: At eight o'clock, Aisha turned off her light. She closed her eyes and took slow breaths. She was asleep in a few minutes.
question: How did Aisha fall asleep fast?
options: Took slow breaths with eyes closed
answer: Took slow breaths with eyes closed

title: A Sunny Day
content: The sun was bright and warm. Lena ran outside to play. She felt the warm sun on her face.
question: How did Lena feel the sun?

title: A Rainy Day
content: Rain fell all morning. Puddles formed on the path. Jo put on her boots and splashed through them.
question: What did Jo do in the rain?

title: The Park
content: The park had a swing, a slide, and a big green field. Children played and laughed. Dogs ran and chased balls.
question: What was in the park?

title: Autumn Leaves
content: In autumn, the leaves turn red, orange, and yellow. They fall from the trees. Children love to jump in the leaf piles.
question: What colour do leaves turn in autumn?

title: Building a Snowman
content: It snowed all night. In the morning, Eli and his mum went outside. They rolled big snowballs and built a snowman.
question: What did Eli and his mum build?

title: The Garden
content: Gran's garden had roses, sunflowers, and strawberries. Gran watered it every morning. Everything grew tall and bright.
question: What was in Gran's garden?

title: The Beach
content: The family went to the beach. The children dug in the sand and found shells. The waves were big and made a loud roar.
question: What did the children find in the sand?

title: Walking in the Rain
content: Dad and Mia walked in the rain with their umbrella. They listened to the raindrops on the umbrella. It made a gentle tapping sound.
question: What did the rain on the umbrella sound like?

title: The Wind
content: A strong wind blew through the trees. Leaves swirled in the air. Pia held onto her hat so it did not blow away.
question: Why did Pia hold her hat?

title: A Rainbow
content: After the rain stopped, the sun came out. A beautiful rainbow appeared in the sky. It had many bright colours.
question: When did the rainbow appear?

title: Breakfast Time
content: In the morning, Ria ate a bowl of porridge with honey. It was warm and sweet. She felt full of energy after eating.
question: What did Ria eat for breakfast?

title: Making a Sandwich
content: Jed took two slices of bread. He spread butter on both pieces. Then he added cheese and a slice of tomato.
question: What did Jed add to his sandwich?

title: Fruit Salad
content: Mum cut up bananas, grapes, and oranges. She put them in a bowl. She poured a little honey on top.
question: What did Mum pour on the fruit?

title: Eating Together
content: Every evening, the family ate dinner at the table together. They talked about their day. It was everyone's favourite time.
question: When did the family eat dinner?

title: Trying New Food
content: Zoe had never tried peas before. She put one in her mouth slowly. It was sweet! She ate them all up.
question: What did Zoe think of the peas?

title: Drinking Water
content: After running around, Dan was very thirsty. He drank a full glass of cold water. He felt much better straight away.
question: Why did Dan drink water?

title: Baking a Cake
content: Noor and her dad made a cake. They mixed flour, eggs, and butter. The cake baked in the oven for thirty minutes.
question: What did they mix for the cake?

title: The Ice Cream Shop
content: On a hot day, Kai went to the ice cream shop. He chose strawberry flavour. He licked it fast before it melted.
question: What flavour did Kai choose?

title: Growing Food
content: Gran planted tomato seeds in a pot. She watered them every day. After a few weeks, small red tomatoes grew.
question: What grew in Gran's pot?

title: Cooking Together
content: Mia helped her dad make soup. She stirred the pot while he cut the vegetables. It smelled wonderful.
question: What did Mia do?

title: The Tiny Dragon
content: A tiny dragon lived under a mushroom. He could only breathe a small puff of smoke. He used it to warm cold cups of tea.
question: What could the tiny dragon do?
options: Breathe a small puff of smoke
answer: Breathe a small puff of smoke

title: The Magic Pencil
content: Leo found a magic pencil. Whatever he drew came to life. He drew a dog, and a dog jumped out of the page.
question: What happened when Leo drew a dog?
options: A dog jumped out of the page
answer: A dog jumped out of the page

title: The Friendly Cloud
content: A small cloud floated low in the sky. It would follow children to school. It made shade so they did not get too hot.
question: Why did the cloud follow children?
options: To block the sun from flowers

title: The Talking Mirror
content: In an old house lived a mirror that could talk. It told the truth always. When a girl asked: Am I kind? it said: Yes, very kind.
question: What did the mirror always do?

title: The Giant's Shoes
content: A giant left his shoes by the river. A family of mice moved in. They made it into a cosy home with tiny doors and windows.
question: What did the mice do with the shoes?

title: The Star That Fell
content: One night, a star fell from the sky into a garden. It glowed softly in the grass. A girl picked it up and it turned into a firefly.
question: What did the star turn into?

title: The Invisible Cape
content: Finn found an invisible cape in his wardrobe. When he wore it, nobody could see him. He used it to surprise his little sister.
question: What did Finn use the cape for?

title: The Singing River
content: Deep in the forest, a river sang soft songs as it flowed. Animals came to listen. They lay down on the bank and fell asleep.
question: What happened to the animals who listened?

title: The Door in the Tree
content: There was a small red door at the base of an old oak tree. Behind it lived a tiny world with tiny streets, shops, and people.
question: What was behind the small red door?

title: The Cloud Castle
content: High above the clouds was a castle made of soft white cloud. The people who lived there walked on fluffy floors. They wore coats of fog.
question: What was the castle made of?

title: The Lost Key
content: Mia could not find her key. She thought carefully about where she last had it. She checked her coat pocket and found it.
question: How did Mia find her key?
options: She thought carefully and checked her coat
answer: She thought carefully and checked her coat

title: The Stuck Lid
content: Tom could not open the jar. He ran it under warm water. Then it opened easily.
question: How did Tom open the jar?
options: He ran it under warm water
answer: He ran it under warm water

title: The Wet Shoes
content: Lila's shoes got soaked in a puddle. She stuffed them with newspaper. By morning, they were dry again.
question: What did Lila use to dry her shoes?

title: The Short Ladder
content: The ladder was too short to reach the shelf. Deb put a strong box on the floor first. Then the ladder reached perfectly.
question: How did Deb reach the shelf?
options: She put a box under the ladder
answer: She put a box under the ladder

title: The Dark Room
content: The torch battery died. Eli did not panic. He used the light on his watch to find the main switch.
question: What did Eli use for light?

title: The Long Walk
content: Jess and her dad had to walk a very long way. To make the time fun, they counted red cars. The walk felt much shorter.
question: What did Jess and her dad do to pass the time?

title: The Broken Crayon
content: Nico's favourite crayon snapped in two. He did not cry. He kept using both pieces to colour.
question: What did Nico do with the broken crayon?

title: The Missing Piece
content: The puzzle had one missing piece. Suki looked under the table. She found it near the leg of the chair.
question: Where was the missing piece?
options: Near the leg of the chair
answer: Near the leg of the chair

title: The Argument
content: Two friends wanted different games. They could not agree. They decided to play one game each and take turns.
question: How did the friends solve the argument?
options: They took turns with each game
answer: They took turns with each game

title: The Uphill Path
content: The hill was very steep. Asha rested every few minutes. Step by step, she made it all the way to the top.
question: How did Asha climb the steep hill?
options: She rested and went step by step
answer: She rested and went step by step

title: Trying Again
content: Finn fell off his bike three times. He got back up each time. On the fourth try, he rode all the way down the street.
question: How many times did Finn fall?

title: A New Word
content: Lila heard the word "curious" in a story. She did not know what it meant. She looked it up and learned it means wanting to know more.
question: What does "curious" mean?

title: Asking for Help
content: The sums were very hard for Sam. He put up his hand and asked his teacher. The teacher explained it clearly and Sam understood.
question: What did Sam do when he was stuck?

title: Practising Every Day
content: Mia wanted to learn to draw a cat. She drew one every day. After ten days, her cat looked great.
question: What did Mia practise every day?

title: Making a Mistake
content: Tom gave the wrong answer in class. He felt embarrassed. Then he listened carefully and got the next question right.
question: What did Tom do after his mistake?
options: Listened and got the next one right
answer: Listened and got the next one right

title: Learning to Read
content: At first, reading was hard for Nia. She sounded out letters one by one. Over time, she could read whole sentences with ease.
question: How did Nia learn to read?

title: Feeling Stuck
content: The painting was not going how Ben wanted. He put it aside and took a break. When he came back, he had a new idea.
question: What helped Ben get a new idea?

title: Growing Braver
content: Isla was shy and did not like speaking in front of the class. She practised at home every night. Soon she loved it.
question: How did Isla become brave?
options: She asked a friend to do it
She practised at home every night
answer: She practised at home every night

title: Reading Together
content: Every night, Mum read one chapter with Liam. He began to remember the words. Soon he started reading the pages himself.
question: What happened after reading every night?

title: Believing in Yourself
content: "I cannot do it," said Aara. "Not yet," said her dad. "But you can learn." Aara tried her best. She did it in the end.
question: What did Aara's dad mean by "not yet"?
options: She can learn if she tries
She needs more time to rest
answer: She can learn if she tries

title: Making a New Friend
content: On the first day, no one talked to Kai. He smiled at the boy next to him. That boy smiled back, and they became best friends.
question: How did Kai make a new friend?
options: He gave the boy a toy
He smiled at the boy next to him
answer: He smiled at the boy next to him

title: A Team Effort
content: The sandcastle kept falling down. All four friends worked together. They held each side, and the castle stood tall.
question: How did the sandcastle stand up?

title: The Shy Girl
content: Rosa never spoke in group games. One day, a boy asked her to join. She nodded yes. She loved the game and made three friends.
question: What happened when Rosa joined the game?
options: She loved it and made friends
answer: She loved it and made friends

title: Keeping a Secret
content: Jay's mum was planning a surprise party for Dad. Jay knew about it. He kept the secret all week and did not say a word.
question: What did Jay keep?

title: The Class Project
content: Each child in the class was given a small job. Together, they made a huge mural. Everyone's work made the big picture complete.
question: What did the class make together?

title: A Good Listener
content: Maya's friend was upset. Maya sat beside her and listened carefully. She did not interrupt even once.
question: What did Maya do for her upset friend?

title: Visiting Grandma
content: Every Sunday, Min visited her grandma. They played cards and baked biscuits. Min always left with a full tummy and a big smile.
question: What did Min and her grandma do?

title: Helping a Neighbour
content: The old lady next door could not carry her shopping. Arjun helped her to the door. She thanked him and gave him an apple.
question: What did Arjun receive for helping?

title: The Birthday Card
content: Ella made a card for her friend's birthday. She drew flowers and wrote a kind message inside. Her friend kept it for a long time.
question: What did Ella draw on the card?

title: Looking Out for Others
content: On the bus, Jude saw an older man with no seat. Jude stood up and offered his seat. Everyone on the bus smiled.
question: What did Jude do on the bus?
options: Offered his seat to the older man
answer: Offered his seat to the older man

title: Taking Deep Breaths
content: Ava felt her heart racing before her test. She closed her eyes and took three deep breaths. She felt much calmer.
question: What helped Ava feel calmer?

title: Getting Enough Sleep
content: Liam always went to bed at eight o'clock. He slept for nine hours. In the morning, he was full of energy.
question: Why was Liam full of energy?

title: Playing Outside
content: Sana played in the garden for one hour after school. She ran, jumped, and climbed. She felt great and her mind felt free.
question: How did Sana feel after playing outside?

title: Talking About Feelings
content: Dan felt very worried but said nothing all day. That evening he told his mum. She listened and he felt much lighter.
question: What helped Dan feel lighter?
options: Telling his mum how he felt
answer: Telling his mum how he felt

title: Eating Colourful Food
content: Nia's mum said, "Eat the rainbow!" Nia ate red tomatoes, orange carrots, and green peas. She felt strong and healthy.
question: What does "eat the rainbow" mean?

title: Asking for a Hug
content: After a hard day, Mia went to her dad. "I need a hug," she said. Her dad hugged her tight. She instantly felt better.
question: What did Mia ask for?

title: Spending Time Alone
content: Eli liked spending time alone with his books. It helped him rest his mind. After quiet time, he felt ready to play with friends.
question: What did Eli do in his quiet time?

title: Saying What You Need
content: Zoe was cold in class but said nothing. The next day she asked, "May I close the window please?" The teacher said yes.
question: What did Zoe do the next day?

title: Being Kind to Yourself
content: Tom made a mistake in the game. He said, "That's okay. I will do better next time." He felt calm and carried on playing.
question: What did Tom tell himself?

title: Celebrating Small Wins
content: Ana wrote three whole sentences all by herself. She clapped for herself. Her teacher gave her a star sticker.
question: What did Ana celebrate?
